perturbation.original_text: In the event that the Disclosee or any of its Representatives becomes legally compelled to disclose any of the Confidential Information to a regulatory authority or to any other entity or third party, the Disclosee shall immediately notify the Sellers before disclosing such Confidential In formation, so that the Sellers may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy, without prejudice to t he Disclosee’s remedies under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that the Disclosee or any of its Representatives becomes legally compelled to disclose any of the Confidential Details to a regulatory authority or to any other entity or third party, the Disclosee shall immediately notify the Sellers before disclosing such Confidential In formation, so that the Sellers may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy, without prejudice to t he Disclosee’s remedies under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Confidential Information' is changed to 'Confidential Details' in this section. As 'Confidential Details' is not explicitly defined, it introduces ambiguity as to whether it encompasses the same scope of information as 'Confidential Information' and may be interpreted narrowly. The in-text contradiction takes place because of this ambiguity, it is not clear whether 'Confidential Information' and 'Confidential Details' are the same and create this lack of clarity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information shall mean the following: a) all such information, of any kind whatsoever (whether in oral, written or electronic form, and including, but not limited to, technical, commercial, financial, accounting, legal and administrative information) pertaining to the Sale of the Munt and the Sell ers as may be provided to the Disclosee and their responsible managers, officers, employees , shareholders, members of the Board of Directors and advisors (including financial, legal and tax advisors and auditors) (“ Representatives ”), by the Seller s, their advisors or their representatives; b) all such analyses, compilations, forecasts, notes, memoranda and other documents as the Seller s or Deloitte Real Estate * and Laga ** (the “ Advisors ”) may have prepared or caused to be prepared and which may contain, refer to or simply result from, the information mentioned in point (a) above; c) the fact that the Disclosee (or any of their Representatives) are or have been involved in the analysis of, in meetings or negotiations related to the Sale , the contents, time and sta tus of such negotiations, and generally any fact concerning the Sale .
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'Confidential Information', which is explicitly defined in the 'Contradicted Text'. The changed text replaces this with 'Confidential Details', which is not defined. This creates a contradiction because it introduces ambiguity regarding what information the Disclosee is obligated to protect, contradicting the clear definition of 'Confidential Information' provided elsewhere in the contract. Because of the lack of clarity on 'Confidential Details', it introduces a strong ambiguity and possible change in the obligations that the Disclosee has, leading to in-text contradiction, as described by the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with Belgian law and the Parties irrevocably agree to submit themselves to the exclusive jurisdiction of the Courts of Brussels.
perturbation.changed_text: This Contract shall be governed by and construed in accordance with Belgian law and the Parties irrevocably agree to submit themselves to the exclusive jurisdiction of the Courts of Brussels.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Agreement' is replaced with 'Contract'. The first line refers to 'Agreement', whereas in the rest of the document 'Agreement' is used. There is no explanation to whether it is referring to the same. This affects legal construction, because it doesn't state the names or titles are interchangeable. This is an in-text contradiction, because it isn't clear that is it referring to the same.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidentiality Agreement This Confidentiality Agreement (the “ Agreement ”) has been prepared on behalf of bpost SA/NV, a limited liability company of public law, and the C ity of Brussels (the “Sellers ”) with regard to the divestment of their respective surface areas (the “ Sale”) in the Munt Center /Centre Monnaie (the “Munt ”). In consideration of the Sellers agreeing to disclose confidential information (the “ Confidential Information ”) to the Candidate (“Disclosee ”), the latter hereby undertakes to the Sellers that as Disclosee , they will keep confidential all and any Confidential Information received at any time. The Disclosee will procure that prior to the disclosure to any other person (including any professional advisor) of any Confidential Information, such other person is made aware of the provisions of this Agreement and the fact that t he Disclosee will be liable.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Agreement' with 'Contract' in a clause that defines governing law. The contradicted text defines 'Agreement' specifically as a 'Confidentiality Agreement' related to a divestment. By changing 'Agreement' to 'Contract' without clarification, the modified text creates ambiguity and contradiction because it is no longer clear whether 'Contract' refers to the same 'Agreement' defined elsewhere. This creates uncertainty regarding the applicable law for the Confidentiality Agreement, leading to potential disputes and is an instance of misaligned terminology as described in the perturbation definitions.

perturbation.original_text: In consideration of the Sellers agreeing to disclose confidential information (the “ Confidential Information ”) to the Candidate (“Disclosee ”), the latter hereby undertakes to the Sellers that as Disclosee , they will keep confidential all and any Confidential Information received at any time.
perturbation.changed_text: In consideration of the Sellers agreeing to disclose confidential data (the “ Confidential Information ”) to the Candidate (“Disclosee ”), the latter hereby undertakes to the Sellers that as Disclosee , they will keep confidential all and any Confidential Information received at any time.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'confidential information' to 'confidential data' in this specific clause creates an in-text contradiction. Although 'Confidential Information' is defined, the prefactory phrase now uses the term 'confidential data' which may be construed as narrower or different, particularly in a legal context. The contradiction is that the introduction speaks of 'confidential data' while the rest speaks of 'Confidential Information', and doesn't define whether the two terms are interchangeable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information shall mean the following: a) all such information, of any kind whatsoever (whether in oral, written or electronic form, and including, but not limited to, technical, commercial, financial, accounting, legal and administrative information) pertaining to the Sale of the Munt and the Sell ers as may be provided to the Disclosee and their responsible managers, officers, employees , shareholders, members of the Board of Directors and advisors (including financial, legal and tax advisors and auditors) (“ Representatives ”), by the Seller s, their advisors or their representatives; b) all such analyses, compilations, forecasts, notes, memoranda and other documents as the Seller s or Deloitte Real Estate * and Laga ** (the “ Advisors ”) may have prepared or caused to be prepared and which may contain, refer to or simply result from, the information mentioned in point (a) above; c) the fact that the Disclosee (or any of their Representatives) are or have been involved in the analysis of, in meetings or negotiations related to the Sale , the contents, time and sta tus of such negotiations, and generally any fact concerning the Sale .
perturbation.justification: The change from 'confidential information' to 'confidential data' introduces a potential ambiguity. Although 'Confidential Information' is explicitly defined in the contradicted text, the introductory phrase in the changed text uses 'confidential data'. The contract does not define 'confidential data' or explicitly state that it is interchangeable with 'Confidential Information.' This creates a potential contradiction because a court could interpret 'confidential data' more narrowly than the defined term 'Confidential Information,' potentially excluding some items covered in the contradicted text's definition, thus causing uncertainty and conflict.

perturbation.original_text: The Disclosee acknowledge s that they may not approach the representatives of the Sellers and cannot contact anybody else other than the contact persons from the Advisors .
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient acknowledge s that they may not approach the representatives of the Sellers and cannot contact anybody else other than the contact persons from the Advisors .
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Disclosee' with 'Recipient' introduces an in-text contradiction. The contract begins by defining the recipient of confidential information as 'Disclosee', but this section switches to 'Recipient'. The rest of the document speaks of 'Disclosee', and creates confusion, the two terms might not be interchangeable. This change creates uncertainty about who is bound by this specific restriction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of the Sellers agreeing to disclose confidential information (the “ Confidential Information ”) to the Candidate (“Disclosee ”), the latter hereby undertakes to the Sellers that as Disclosee , they will keep confidential all and any Confidential Information received at any time. The Disclosee will procure that prior to the disclosure to any other person (including any professional advisor) of any Confidential Information, such other person is made aware of the provisions of this Agreement and the fact that t he Disclosee will be liable.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'Disclosee' which is explicitly defined in the 'Contradicted Text'. The changed text replaces 'Disclosee' with 'Recipient', while the rest of the contract, including the contradicted text, refers to 'Disclosee'. This introduces a Misaligned Terminology as defined in the Perturbation Definitions, creating confusion and potential unenforceability regarding who is bound by the confidentiality obligations. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: In the event of an actual or threatened breach by the Disclosee and/or its Representatives of any provisions of this Agreement , the Sellers shall be entitled (i) t o seek injunctive relief in any court of competent jurisdiction restraining the Disclosee and/or its Representatives from breaching the terms hereof or from disclosing any Confidential Information to any person and (ii) specific performance and other equit able relief.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event of an actual or threatened breach by the Candidate and/or its Representatives of any provisions of this Agreement , the Sellers shall be entitled (i) t o seek injunctive relief in any court of competent jurisdiction restraining the Disclosee and/or its Representatives from breaching the terms hereof or from disclosing any Confidential Information to any person and (ii) specific performance and other equit able relief.
perturbation.explanation: The contract refers to the recipient as 'Disclosee' throughout the text, but this paragraph uses 'Candidate' in one spot, then switches back to 'Disclosee'. The term are not interchangeable and creates legal uncertainty, because it is never stated that the 'Candidate' and 'Disclosee' mean the same thing. This in-text contradiction creates the problem of whether the clause applied to both, or only the Disclosee and that an action taken under the Candidate isn't subject to action under this paragraph.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of the Sellers agreeing to disclose confidential information (the “ Confidential Information ”) to the Candidate (“Disclosee ”), the latter hereby undertakes to the Sellers that as Disclosee , they will keep confidential all and any Confidential Information received at any time.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Disclosee' with 'Candidate' in one instance within a paragraph otherwise referring to 'Disclosee'. This creates ambiguity and a direct conflict with the 'Contradicted Text' which defines 'Disclosee' and their obligations regarding confidential information. The change introduces a 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' because the two terms are used without defining them as equivalent, creating uncertainty about whether the clause applies to both entities and potentially conflicting obligations based on which term is used.
